### Reinforcement Learning

Among the machine learning paradigms discussed above, reinforcement learning is
the most directly aligned with trading as a sequential decision-making problem:
an agent observes the market state, takes an action (e.g., adjusts exposure),
and receives a reward that may reflect both return and risk. In contrast to
supervised learning (prediction from labeled examples) and unsupervised learning
(structure discovery), reinforcement learning optimizes behavior through
interaction under delayed rewards and uncertainty. For this reason, the thesis
uses reinforcement learning as the core modelling framework, while the formal
theoretical foundations (system components, value functions, exploration, and
algorithm categories) are developed in detail in Chapter 4.
